name: Black Pepper

book_claims.claim: Contains piperine which doubles blood curcumin levels when combined with turmeric
book_claims.mechanism: Piperine inhibits metabolic clearance of curcumin, improving bioavailability

supplementary_research.finding: Landmark study showing piperine (20 mg) increased curcumin bioavailability by 2000% in humans. Piperine inhibits hepatic and intestinal glucuronidation, the primary pathway for curcumin elimination.

Comprehensive review found piperine demonstrates anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties independent of its bioavailability-enhancing role. Also improves absorption of selenium, vitamin B6, and beta-carotene.

Piperine acts as a general bioenhancer by inhibiting drug metabolism enzymes (CYP3A4, CYP1A2) and P-glycoprotein efflux pumps. This mechanism enhances bioavailability of multiple compounds beyond curcumin, including resveratrol and EGCG from green tea.

culinary_pairings.source: Piperine doubles curcumin bioavailability; foundational in curry blends
Cacio e pepe; fat carrier for piperine and enhanced compound absorption
Acid and heat create flavour contrast in dressings and marinades
Universal aromatic pairing across virtually all savoury cuisines
Both warming spices; combined in chai, curries, and stir-fries
Piperine enhances EGCG bioavailability from green tea

seasonality: Available year-round as dried peppercorns. Freshly ground black pepper retains volatile oils and piperine far better than pre-ground. Whole peppercorns have a shelf life of 3-4 years; ground pepper degrades within months.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 5-9 g (5-9% of black pepper by weight)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Rapidly absorbed. Inhibits CYP3A4, CYP1A2, and P-glycoprotein. This is a double-edged sword: also enhances absorption of medications, so caution with pharmaceutical drugs.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Major terpene in pepper essential oil
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Dietary cannabinoid that activates CB2 receptors. Anti-inflammatory without psychoactive effects.
One of the richest food sources of manganese, though typical serving sizes are small.

synergies.description: Piperine doubles blood curcumin levels by inhibiting glucuronidation
Piperine enhances bioavailability of EGCG from green tea
